global_kris.logic_summary: Measures the rate of protocol deviations per participant in study C4891023. This includes deviations related to ARV-471 and ribociclib dosing, missed safety assessments like QTc monitoring, or out-of-window visits during the 28-day cycles.
global_kris.corrective_action: Review the Protocol Deviation eCRF and CTMS logs to identify trends. Retrain the site on the C4891023 Schedule of Activities, particularly regarding the reduced SoA implemented in Amendment 4 and strict QTc monitoring requirements.

global_kris.logic_summary: Calculates the number of adverse events reported per participant, focusing on the safety profile of the ARV-471 and ribociclib combination. Key safety endpoints include DLTs during the Cycle 1 observation period and QTc prolongation events.
global_kris.corrective_action: Review the Adverse Event eCRF to ensure accurate grading per NCI CTCAE v5.0. If the threshold is breached, verify if AEs are related to study drugs and ensure prompt reporting of DLTs to the Sponsor medical monitor.

global_kris.logic_summary: Measures the rate of serious adverse events per participant in this advanced/metastatic breast cancer population. This tracks critical safety signals for the ARV-471 and ribociclib combination across all treatment cycles.
global_kris.corrective_action: Cross-check the SAE eCRF against the safety database for reconciliation. Contact the site to ensure timely reporting of SAEs and verify if any dose modifications or interruptions for ARV-471 or ribociclib were properly documented.

global_kris.logic_summary: Calculates the percentage of participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC who terminate study participation early. This includes dropouts during the ARV-471 Lead-In Period or early combination treatment cycles before reaching efficacy evaluation.
global_kris.corrective_action: Analyze the Disposition eCRF to identify if early terminations are driven by DLTs or other AEs. Engage the site to understand root causes and ensure all end-of-treatment procedures are completed per the Amendment 4 reduced SoA.

study_specific_kris.iqmp_risk_id: PK data is secondary endpoint for the study (KRI_ MISSED_PK_POINTS) and is critical data per IQMP.
study_specific_kris.logic_summary: Tracks the proportion of missed PK blood draws for ARV-471, ARV-473, and ribociclib. This is critical for evaluating the steady-state AUCtau, Cmax, and potential drug-drug interactions between the two study drugs.
study_specific_kris.corrective_action: Review the PK sample collection logs and eCRF for missed timepoints, especially during the ARV-471 Lead-In Period and Cycle 1. Retrain the site on the specific PK collection windows and processing manual to prevent further missed samples.

study_specific_kris.logic_summary: Calculates the percentage of participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC who terminate study participation early. This includes dropouts during the ARV-471 Lead-In Period or early combination treatment cycles before reaching efficacy evaluation.
study_specific_kris.corrective_action: Analyze the Disposition eCRF to identify if early terminations are driven by DLTs or other AEs. Engage the site to understand root causes and ensure all end-of-treatment procedures are completed per the Amendment 4 reduced SoA.

study_specific_kris.logic_summary: Measures the proportion of missed or delayed tumor assessments per RECIST v1.1. These assessments are essential for evaluating secondary efficacy endpoints like Objective Response Rate, Duration of Response, and Progression-Free Survival.
study_specific_kris.corrective_action: Check the Tumor Assessment eCRF and imaging logs for missing scans. Contact the site to schedule makeup scans immediately and retrain staff on the required imaging frequency for evaluating measurable lesions in this A/MBC population.
